ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT code 227313005 refers to green tea as a substance; used as closest parent for brewed green tea beverage.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard cup of brewed green tea, approximately 240 ml, as typically served at a tea house or café.

nutrient_source.title: Tea, hot, leaf, green — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #14355
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Values sourced from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry #14355 for brewed green tea (plain, no additives). This is an exact match for the base food. HPB Singapore Foodo was consulted, but no direct entry for plain, unsweetened, freshly brewed green tea was found; available HPB entries were for sweetened or bottled versions, which do not accurately represent this product. Therefore, USDA SR Legacy, an approved secondary global source for generic staples, was used as the highest-priority valid source.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Beverages — Plain brewed green tea contains negligible fermentable carbohydrates; GI conventionally assigned as effectively zero
gi_source.source_match_notes: Plain brewed green tea contains approximately 0.2 g carbohydrate per 100 ml with no glycemic carbohydrates. The University of Sydney GI Database, the primary approved GI source, does not list a direct GI value for plain green tea because it contains insufficient carbohydrate to elicit a measurable glycemic response. By convention for essentially zero-carbohydrate beverages, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel value) and gi_category is 'low'. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 as carbohydrate content per serving is negligible. HPB Singapore Foodo does not provide GI values, and the University of Sydney GI Database is the authoritative source for GI.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (1 mg per 100 ml); contains catechins with mild vasodilatory properties
hypertension.guidance: Plain brewed green tea is appropriate for individuals with hypertension due to its negligible sodium content (approximately 2.4 mg per 240 ml serving) and evidence from observational studies suggesting regular green tea consumption may modestly support blood pressure management.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Negligible carbohydrate content; glycemic index effectively zero; polyphenols may improve insulin sensitivity
type2_diabetes.guidance: Plain brewed green tea is well-suited for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes as it contains negligible carbohydrate (approximately 0.5 g per 240 ml serving), exerts no meaningful glycemic load, and epidemiological evidence suggests green tea catechins (EGCG) may improve insulin sensitivity when consumed regularly without added sugar.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Zero saturated fat; rich in catechins and antioxidants; associated with reduced LDL oxidation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Plain brewed green tea is considered cardioprotective; it contains no saturated fat or cholesterol, and its high catechin content (particularly EGCG) has been associated in meta-analyses with modest reductions in LDL cholesterol and improved endothelial function when consumed as part of a balanced diet.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Green tea (绿茶, lǜ chá) is a minimally processed beverage made by steeping the dried leaves of Camellia sinensis in hot water. Unlike black or oolong tea, green tea leaves are not oxidised after harvesting, which preserves their natural green colour, delicate grassy or vegetal flavour, and high concentration of polyphenolic antioxidants, particularly epigallocatechin gallate (EGCG). The item listed here — 松针绿茶 (Pine Needle Green Tea) — is served at CHICHA San Chen, a Taiwanese specialty tea brand, and is described as combining the freshness of green tea with a delicate jasmine scent, leaving a lasting sweet and refreshing finish. As a plain brewed tea, it is essentially calorie-free (approximately 1 kcal per 100 ml), contains negligible carbohydrate, no fat, and no cholesterol. It is naturally vegan, gluten-free, halal, and kosher. Clinically, plain green tea is considered beneficial for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease due to its negligible glycemic load, very low sodium, and antioxidant polyphenol content. Regular consumption has been associated in epidemiological studies with modest improvements in blood pressure, insulin sensitivity, and LDL cholesterol profiles. Green tea is a staple beverage across East and Southeast Asia and is widely consumed in Singapore as both a traditional and modern café drink.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for plain brewed green tea, which is an exact match for the base food. GI is set by convention for a negligible-carbohydrate beverage consistent with University of Sydney GI Database methodology. Ontology codes are verified against FoodOn and FoodEx2 catalogues. Explicit justification for using USDA over HPB Singapore Foodo is provided in source_match_notes.
notes: 1. fat_g is 0.0: naturally zero — plain brewed green tea contains no fat by nature; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy #14355. 2. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are 0.0: naturally zero for a fat-free brewed beverage. 3. sugar_g is 0.0: naturally zero for plain unsweetened brewed green tea; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central. 4. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: naturally zero in the brewed liquid; fibre remains in the leaf residue. 5. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: naturally zero — plant-derived beverage contains no cholesterol. 6. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b12_mcg, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg are 0.0: confirmed zero or below detection threshold per USDA FoodData Central for brewed green tea. 7. glycemic_index set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains no glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category low per convention for negligible-carbohydrate beverages. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 since carbohydrate per serving is negligible. 8. The field survey item name 'Green Tea松针绿茶' includes the Chinese name 松针绿茶 (Pine Needle Green Tea), a specialty product of CHICHA San Chen. The base nutrient profile is modelled on plain brewed green tea as no added sugar or milk is indicated in the product description. If the commercial product contains added sugar or other ingredients, values should be updated from the brand's nutritional disclosure. 9. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g are 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA source for brewed green tea). 10. food_group updated from input 'Mixed' to 'Beverages' as this is a tea beverage. 11. All -1 sentinel values are not used; replaced with 0.0 where confirmed zero per source, or actual numeric values where available.
